festivals.name: Inti Raymi
festivals.description: The Inca Festival of the Sun is Peru's greatest pageant, re-enacted at the fortress of Sacsayhuaman above Cusco.

festivals.name: Virgen de la Candelaria
festivals.description: Peru's largest festival explodes around Lake Titicaca with 40,000 dancers and hundreds of brass bands in elaborate costumes.

destinations.name: Machu Picchu
destinations.description: The Lost City of the Incas perched on a cloud-forest ridge at 2,430m — one of the world's great archaeological sites.

destinations.name: Cusco
destinations.description: The former Inca capital merges Spanish baroque churches with ancient Inca stonework in a high-altitude city.

destinations.name: Sacred Valley
destinations.description: The fertile valley between Pisac and Ollantaytambo is studded with Inca terraces, ruins, and indigenous markets.

destinations.name: Lima
destinations.description: Peru's capital is a world-class food destination with a historic centre, dramatic clifftop views, and pre-Columbian museums.

destinations.name: Lake Titicaca
destinations.description: The world's highest navigable lake at 3,812m, home to the floating Uros islands and traditional Taquile weavers.

destinations.name: Arequipa
destinations.description: The White City of gleaming sillar-stone architecture sits below three volcanoes, including the active El Misti.

destinations.name: Colca Canyon
destinations.description: One of the world's deepest canyons is the best place on earth to watch Andean condors soar at eye level.

destinations.name: Huacachina
destinations.description: A real desert oasis surrounded by towering sand dunes — Peru's most dramatic sandboarding and dune-buggy destination.
